rationale: The cited paper is used in the theory-building section to support a specific mechanism: hedonic options can gain from a utilitarian 'token' that makes indulgent choice easier to justify. This citation is tied to the citing paper's core argument that more attributes increase perceived usefulness and thereby shift choices toward hedonic options. Although it is not methodologically reused or extensively discussed, it plays a meaningful conceptual role in framing the justification pathway behind the hypotheses.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Urminsky and Kivetz (2011) as prior conceptual support that small utilitarian cues can legitimize hedonic choices. It builds its usefulness/justification account in part on this idea while applying it to attribute numerosity in choice sets.
